{{Quote|Recall the dying burble of Asenec of Crakull: 'Tyrants, however small, truckle to no one.'|[[Zeta Magnus]], to [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]|<ref name="SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>}}
'''Crakull''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="NEGtC" /> located within the [[Crakull system/Legends|Crakull system]] of the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was home to the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Croke]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="NEGtC" /> The Croke were diminutive in size, and had multiple [[Limb/Legends|legs]] as well as a natural ability to disguise themselves behind [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]].<ref name="ThonBoka">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]''</ref>

[[Rokur Gepta]] was a Croke who was born on Crakull<ref name="NEGtC" /> at least twenty [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before<ref name="ThonBoka" /> the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],<ref name="date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' to the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].</ref> while the Croke [[Poet/Legends|poet]] [[Asenec]] was born there in [[593 BBY]].<ref name="Xim">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-3-xim-the-despot|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 3 - "Xim the Despot"}}</ref> At some point, the Croke settled planets in the region known as the [[Croke Reach]], including the world [[Explume Minor]].<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Crakull was first mentioned in the [[2002]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="NEGtC" /> The Crakull [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, was placed in grid square G-10 by the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
